title: System Visibility

content.statement: You can't improve what you can't see.
content.question: Which system are you operating blind on right now?
sop: List the 3 core metrics that actually indicate whether your business is healthy.
Rate your current Visibility 1-10 into each, can you see the real-time number or not (see calculation, page 5)
Build a simple dashboard or tracker for anything currently invisible.
Rate your Understanding 1-10, do you actually know what a good vs bad number looks like (see calculation, page 5)
Review the dashboard on a fixed weekly cadence, not just when something feels off.
questions: Which system are you operating blind on right now?
What number about your business could you not tell someone right now without looking it up?
What decision did you make this month based on a guess instead of a number?

definitions.def: Key Performance Indicator, a measurable value that shows how effectively you're achieving a key business objective.

content.equation_example: Rate Visibility 1-10 (how much of the system you can actually see) and Understanding 1-10 (how well you interpret what you see). Visibility 7 times Understanding 6 equals 42 out of 100. Higher means you can act accurately on what's really happening. Lower means you're either blind to the data or misreading it.
content.closing_statement: A number you never check isn't managing anything.

caption:
Principle Nº 146: System Visibility

You can't improve what you can't see.

Ask yourself: Which system are you operating blind on right now?
